name: SUBDURAL HYGROMA
definition: TRAUMATIC SUBDURAL EFFUSION localized CSF-fluid collection within subdural space

demographics.Age: most often in elderly + young children

dominant_finding: isointense to CSF / hyperintense to CSF on T1WI (← increased protein content)
discriminating_features: radiolucent crescent-shaped collection (as in acute subdural hematoma) of CSF density
NO evidence of blood products (DDx to subdural hematoma)
isointense to CSF / hyperintense to CSF on T1WI (← increased protein content)

general: radiolucent crescent-shaped collection (as in acute subdural hematoma) of CSF density
NO evidence of blood products (DDx to subdural hematoma)
MR: isointense to CSF / hyperintense to CSF on T1WI (← increased protein content)

clinical.Prognosis: often spontaneous resorption; may develop into a chronic subdural hematoma
